branch.conclusion: Emit `recoveryHint: RETRY_LATER` with `retry_after_seconds` from the upstream `Retry-After` header or a sane default (5-30s).
Emit `recoveryHint: CHECK_INPUT` — model rereads the arguments and tries again on its own.
Emit `recoveryHint: NEEDS_AUTH` — runner halts the loop and requests credentials from the user.
Emit `recoveryHint: REPORT_TO_USER` — runner halts; do NOT use `RETRY_LATER` as a safety blanket.
Emit `recoveryHint: TRY_ALTERNATIVE` — runner returns to the model and signals that a different tool is available.
Strip stack frames from `message`; move sanitised upstream status to `details`. Do not ship raw traces back to the model.
Replace HTTP-as-code with a semantic UPPER_SNAKE code: `429 → UPSTREAM_RATE_LIMITED`, `401 → AUTH_REQUIRED`, `404 → RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND`.

example.p: Upstream GitHub returns 429 with `Retry-After: 60`. Match → `rate-limit` branch → `RETRY_LATER`. Envelope: `{code: "UPSTREAM_RATE_LIMITED", message: "GitHub API returned 429.", recoveryHint: "RETRY_LATER", retry_after_seconds: 60, traceId: "01HX..."}`.
